threshold_rationale: 300 SCFM is the upper end of standard medium-industrial rotary screw compressors; above this, only large industrial units apply, but not infeasible
delivery_pressure_note: Delivery pressure is 40.66 psia (25.97 psig). This is within the rated range of standard single-stage reciprocating and rotary-screw compressors (typical max 100-175 psig for these classes). No special high-pressure equipment is required.
pipe_friction_note: This analysis assumes ideal air delivery with no line losses. Real installation adds 10-20% to pump energy for pipe friction, check valves, and nozzle losses. Phase 4 COP calculation will include a pipe friction factor of 1.10-1.20 x W_adia / eta_c. This does NOT affect fill feasibility -- a larger compressor rating margin already covers line losses. (Pitfall M5)
verdict_note: All velocity cases are commercially feasible. Fill system is not a blocking constraint.

forbidden_proxy_notes.not_Q_depth_as_compressor_rating: Compressor rating MUST be in SCFM (free air). Q_depth_CFM is flow at depth pressure; specifying a compressor by Q_depth_CFM would undersize it by factor P_r = 2.77.
forbidden_proxy_notes.not_fill_feasibility_as_COP_contribution: Fill feasibility answers: can we physically fill the vessel in time? The compressor energy is entirely on the INPUT side of the COP balance. It does NOT contribute energy to the output side.
forbidden_proxy_notes.pipe_friction_separate: Pipe friction is a Phase 4 COP correction (10-20% on W_pump), not a fill feasibility blocker.
